description:
You are the release manager for a fictitious software project.  
    A set of version-labelled directories is already present in 
    /home/user/releases/.  Each directory contains:
      • CHANGELOG.txt   (plain text)  
      • RELEASE_NOTES.md (Markdown)  

    Goal: build two artefacts in /home/user/deployment/:

    1) /home/user/deployment/release_manifest.csv  
       Exact required format (comma-separated values, RFC-4180 compliant):  
       • First (header) line must be literally:  
         version,changelog_title,release_notes_heading  
       • Subsequent lines: one per release directory, **sorted lexicographically
         by directory name**, newest versions do NOT need to appear first.  
       • Each data line contains three fields:  
         Field-1: the directory name itself (e.g. v2.0.0)  
         Field-2: the first line of the CHANGELOG.txt file, kept verbatim but 
                  wrapped in double quotes even if it contains no commas.  
         Field-3: the first Markdown heading line in RELEASE_NOTES.md that 
                  begins with exactly “# ” (hash, space).  Remove the leading 
                  “# ” when writing the value and wrap the field in double 
                  quotes.  
       • Example line (spacing important only inside the quoted region):  
         v1.0.0,"v1.0.0 - 2022-12-01 Initial release","Release Notes for v1.0.0"

    2) /home/user/deployment/deploy_commands.sh  
       • First line must be the shebang:  #!/bin/bash  
       • For every version appearing in release_manifest.csv, append one 
         command line in the exact form:  
           ./deploy --version <version> --notes release_notes/<version>.md  
       • The lines must appear in the same order as they do in the CSV.  
       • End the file with a trailing newline.  No extra blank lines.

    Additional constraints:  
    • /home/user/deployment/ may not exist yet; create it if necessary.  
    • All files you create must be readable by everyone but writable only by 
      you (octal permissions 644 for the CSV and 755 for the shell script).  
    • Do not alter any existing files inside /home/user/releases/.  
    • You may use any standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities available in 
      a typical Debian/Ubuntu environment, and you may chain commands with 
      pipes and background operators if you deem it efficient.  
    • When finished, list the contents of /home/user/deployment/ so the 
      assessor can see the two new artefacts.

    Hint: parsing the first heading line of the Markdown file can be done with 
    a combination of grep, head, cut/awk, or similar classic tools.  
    Remember to handle directories whose names include non-numeric qualifiers 
    such as “-beta”.
truth:
<!--
    Initial directory tree and file contents already present before the task starts.
    The automated tester will create these exactly; the agent must NOT modify them.
    -->
    /home/user/releases/
        v2.0.0/
            CHANGELOG.txt
                v2.0.0 - 2023-05-10 Major release

                * Added multi-tenant support
                * Deprecated legacy API
            RELEASE_NOTES.md
                # Release Notes for v2.0.0

                ## Highlights
                - Full RBAC implementation
        v2.1.0/
            CHANGELOG.txt
                v2.1.0 - 2023-08-12 Minor release

                * Improved logging
            RELEASE_NOTES.md
                # Release Notes for v2.1.0

                ## Improvements
                - Faster cache invalidation
        v3.0.0-beta/
            CHANGELOG.txt
                v3.0.0-beta - 2024-01-20 Beta release

                * Brand-new micro-kernel
            RELEASE_NOTES.md
                # Release Notes for v3.0.0-beta

                ## Beta caveats
                - May eat your data

    <!--
    Expected artefacts AFTER the agent completes the task.
    Paths must exist with the following exact contents and permissions:
    -->
    /home/user/deployment/               (directory, 755)
        release_manifest.csv             (file, 644)
            version,changelog_title,release_notes_heading
            v2.0.0,"v2.0.0 - 2023-05-10 Major release","Release Notes for v2.0.0"
            v2.1.0,"v2.1.0 - 2023-08-12 Minor release","Release Notes for v2.1.0"
            v3.0.0-beta,"v3.0.0-beta - 2024-01-20 Beta release","Release Notes for v3.0.0-beta"
        deploy_commands.sh               (file, 755)
            #!/bin/bash
            ./deploy --version v2.0.0 --notes release_notes/v2.0.0.md
            ./deploy --version v2.1.0 --notes release_notes/v2.1.0.md
            ./deploy --version v3.0.0-beta --notes release_notes/v3.0.0-beta.md

    <!--
    Verification procedure used by the automated test:
    1. Confirm /home/user/deployment/ exists and is a directory.
    2. Confirm both files exist with correct permissions.
    3. Diff the contents of each file against the exact blocks above.
       Any deviation—including missing quotes, wrong order, or missing
       trailing newline—fails the test.
    -->
